title: Peking Duck

seo.title: Authentic Peking Duck Recipe - Crispy & Delicious at Home
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Peking Duck at home with our easy, step-by-step recipe. Achieve crispy skin and tender meat for a truly delicious Chinese dinner. Perfect for special occasions!
seo.h1: Authentic Peking Duck Recipe: Crispy Skin, Tender Meat
h2Headings: Pro Tips for Perfect Peking Duck
seo.imageAlt: A whole roasted authentic Peking Duck with crispy skin, served with pancakes, scallions, and hoisin sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Master the art of making authentic Peking Duck at home with our detailed recipe, promising crispy skin and succulent meat.

description: Peking Duck is a legendary Chinese delicacy, celebrated for its incredibly crispy skin and succulent, flavorful meat. Traditionally served with thin pancakes, fresh scallions, and a rich hoisin sauce, it offers a delightful textural and taste experience. This iconic dish is a centerpiece for any special occasion, bringing the authentic taste of Beijing to your table.

instructions.title: Prepare the Duck
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean the duck, removing any excess fat, neck, and giblets. Pat it completely dry inside and out with paper towels. Bring 4 cups of water to a rolling boil in a large pot. Carefully lower the duck into the boiling water for 1-2 minutes to blanch the skin. Remove and pat dry again. This step helps tighten the skin for crispiness.

instructions.title: Air Dry the Duck
instructions.text: Hang the duck in a cool, well-ventilated area, preferably with a fan, for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight in the refrigerator. This crucial step allows the skin to dry completely, which is essential for achieving that signature crispy texture. Ensure no moisture remains on the skin.

instructions.title: Prepare the Glaze
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the honey, rice vinegar, dark soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, five-spice powder, and salt until well combined. This mixture will give the duck its rich color and flavor.

instructions.title: Glaze and Dry Again
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 375F (190C). Evenly brush the prepared glaze all over the duck, ensuring every part of the skin is coated. Hang the duck again for another 30 minutes to allow the glaze to set and dry slightly before roasting. This helps create a beautiful, even crust.

instructions.title: Roast the Duck
instructions.text: Place the duck on a rack set over a roasting pan (to catch drippings). Roast in the preheated oven for 30-35 minutes, or until the skin is deeply golden brown and very crispy. Periodically check to ensure it is not burning. The internal temperature should reach 165F (74C) in the thickest part of the thigh.

instructions.title: Rest, Carve, and Serve
instructions.text: Once roasted, remove the duck from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, keeping the meat tender. Carve the crispy skin and meat into thin slices. Serve immediately with warm mandarin pancakes, julienned scallions, julienned cucumber, and a generous amount of hoisin sauce.

tips: For the crispiest skin, ensure the duck is thoroughly air-dried before roasting. Hanging it in front of a fan or in a well-ventilated area overnight is key.
When carving, separate the crispy skin from the meat first. Many prefer to eat the skin separately or with a small amount of meat.
Always warm the mandarin pancakes before serving. You can steam them in a bamboo steamer or microwave them briefly wrapped in a damp paper towel.
article: Peking Duck is a beloved Chinese Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
